behaviors.description: A request whose signature does not match its body is rejected with a 401 response; the body is not forwarded.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: failure is at handler->queue handoff (reject must prevent enqueue), not just pure HMAC math. REAL-INFRA: no real OS/network/subprocess required; in-process request invocation is enough. LLM-DEP/MULTI-STEP: none. Rejected higher level: behavior text only requires '401' and 'not forwarded', with no real HTTP/network requirement.
behaviors.test_description: Wire handler + real signature verifier + queue adapter test double/spied module. Send body/header mismatch, assert HTTP 401 and zero enqueue calls.
behaviors.plan_consistent_note: Test exercises cross-module rejection path; unit-only signature test would miss accidental enqueue.

behaviors.description: An event is written to the persistent queue before the HTTP 200 acknowledgment is returned to the sender.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: ordering bug appears at handler<->queue boundary (ack before durable write). REAL-INFRA: local persistence module can be exercised in-process; no real network/subprocess required. LLM-DEP/MULTI-STEP: none. Rejected System: behavior text specifies ordering with 'persistent queue' but not real HTTP/network conditions.
behaviors.test_description: Use handler wired to real persistent queue module (local test storage). Instrument queue write completion and response completion timestamps; assert write completes before 200 is emitted.
behaviors.plan_consistent_note: Requires module interaction and ordering; unit test of handler alone is insufficient.

behaviors.description: The handler returns the HTTP response without waiting for downstream processing of the event to finish.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: this is async orchestration between handler, queue, and downstream processor. REAL-INFRA: can be reproduced with in-process delayed downstream worker; no OS/network/subprocess dependency required. LLM-DEP/MULTI-STEP: none. Rejected System: behavior text is about wait semantics, not real network behavior.
behaviors.test_description: Wire handler + queue + downstream consumer where consumer blocks on a controllable promise. Assert response is returned before consumer resolves.
behaviors.plan_consistent_note: Test directly validates non-blocking boundary; lower level would miss orchestration regression.

behaviors.description: The response arrives within 3 seconds wall-clock time even when downstream processing of earlier events is slow and concurrent.
behaviors.justification: REAL-INFRA: explicit wall-clock SLA under concurrency depends on runtime scheduling, socket handling, and actual HTTP server behavior. MOCK-HIDE: in-process fakes often hide queueing/connection/event-loop contention. LLM-DEP/MULTI-STEP: none. Rejected Integration: behavior text requires 'within 3 seconds wall-clock' under concurrent load, which is an environment/runtime effect.
behaviors.test_description: Run real service process and issue concurrent HTTP requests while earlier events trigger slow downstream work. Measure end-to-end response latency and assert each response <=3s.
behaviors.plan_consistent_note: Uses real HTTP timing/concurrency, matching the chosen System boundary.

behaviors.description: An event whose ID was already received is not written to the queue a second time.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: dedupe correctness depends on state handoff between handler and persistent queue/repository. REAL-INFRA: local persistence in-process is sufficient; no real OS/network/subprocess required. LLM-DEP/MULTI-STEP: none. Rejected System: behavior text only needs duplicate-ID state behavior, not real external infra.
behaviors.test_description: With handler + real queue storage, send same event ID twice. Assert first is accepted/enqueued, second is not written (idempotent outcome).
behaviors.plan_consistent_note: State-sharing across modules is exercised; pure unit logic would miss persistence/index bugs.

behaviors.description: When the queue reports it is full, the handler returns 503 with a Retry-After header.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: behavior is mapping of queue backpressure signal to HTTP contract across module boundary. REAL-INFRA: no real network/subprocess required; can simulate full condition via queue module behavior. LLM-DEP/MULTI-STEP: none. Rejected System: behavior text says 'queue reports it is full' (interface-level condition), not real infra failure mode.
behaviors.test_description: Wire handler with queue implementation/config that deterministically returns 'full'. Assert HTTP 503 and presence/value format of Retry-After header.
behaviors.plan_consistent_note: Cross-module error propagation is verified; unit-only controller test risks missing integration contract mismatch.

behaviors.description: The persistent queue actually enforces its configured capacity limit and rejects events that would exceed it.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: this validates real queue + persistence logic, where fake queue would always pass. REAL-INFRA: can be tested with actual local DB/filesystem-backed queue in-process; no external network/subprocess required. LLM-DEP/MULTI-STEP: none. Rejected System: behavior text targets capacity enforcement in queue component, not OS/network timing/process boundaries.
behaviors.test_description: Instantiate real persistent queue with small configured capacity. Enqueue until limit, then enqueue one more and assert explicit rejection/no extra persisted item.
behaviors.plan_consistent_note: Test uses actual queue implementation and persistence path, matching Integration scope.
